trades.market: Arsenal FC vs. Club Atlético de Madrid: Both Teams to Score
trades.research_summary: CL semi-final 2nd leg with 1-1 aggregate. Both teams need goals — Arsenal at Emirates will push hard, Atlético dangerous on counters. Recent CL semi-final BTTS rate ~65-70% (7/10 in last 5 semis). 50-50 pricing underestimates the offensive dynamics of a level-aggregate knockout.

Arsenal extremely strong at Emirates — likely to score at least once
Atlético's counter-attacking style historically produces away goals in CL knockouts
Historical BTTS rate in CL semi-finals ~65-70%
trades.bear_case: Atlético parks the bus under Simeone, plays for 0-0 and extra time/penalties. Arsenal controls possession but can't convert. 0-0 or 1-0 Arsenal are plausible low-scoring outcomes.

trades.market: Will Reform UK win at least 2200 council seat elections in the 2026 United Kingdom local elections?
trades.research_summary: Reform won 804 seats in 2025 with limited candidate coverage. In 2026 they're standing in 95%+ of wards with significantly higher polling. YouGov MRP projects them topping polls in all 13 West Midlands council areas. Near-universal coverage alone could double seats; combined with polling surge, 2200+ threshold is achievable though uncertain.
key_factors: 95%+ ward coverage in 2026 vs much less in 2025 — massively more opportunities
National polling surge with YouGov MRP showing regional dominance
804 to 2200 requires 2.7x increase — large but plausible with coverage + polling gains
Low volume ($9,872) suggests limited sophisticated pricing
trades.bear_case: FPTP local elections can be highly inefficient for vote-to-seat conversion; different councils contested in 2026 may be less favorable; no quantitative seat projections available to confirm trajectory; 2.7x jump is historically large

trades.market: Will Bitcoin dip to $81,000 on May 6?
trades.research_summary: BTC opened at $80,900.77 on May 6 per Yahoo Finance, which is below the $81,000 threshold. Standard prediction market resolution for 'dip to X' means price reaches/touches X at any point during the specified day. Since BTC was already below $81K at the daily open, this condition appears already satisfied. Current price $81,342 with -0.22% 24h change. Market at 72¢ has not fully priced in the opening price data.
key_factors: BTC opened at $80,900.77 on May 6 — already below $81,000 threshold
Standard 'dip to' resolution = price touches level at any point during day
Even under alternative interpretation requiring fresh decline, only 0.42% drop needed from $81,342 in ~4.5hrs — well within BTC intraday volatility
trades.bear_case: Resolution criteria may differ from standard interpretation (e.g., specific snapshot time, closing price, or require decline FROM above). If resolution requires BTC to close at/below $81K, current price of $81,342 makes this uncertain. Market at 72% rather than 95%+ suggests possible resolution nuance.
